From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Skinhead Moonstomp](/w/index.php?title=Skinhead_Moonstomp&redirect=no "Skinhead Moonstomp"))

1969 single by Derrick Morgan

| "Moon Hop" | |
| --- | --- |
| [Single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") by [Derrick Morgan](/wiki/Derrick_Morgan "Derrick Morgan") | |
| [A-side](/wiki/A-side_and_B-side "A-side and B-side") | "Moon Hop" |
| [B-side](/wiki/A-side_and_B-side "A-side and B-side") | "[Harris Wheel](/w/index.php?title=Harris_Wheel&action=edit&redlink=1 "Harris Wheel (page does not exist)")" ([Reggaeites](/w/index.php?title=Reggaeites&action=edit&redlink=1 "Reggaeites (page does not exist)"))[[1]](#cite_note-discogs-1) |
| Released | 1969[[1]](#cite_note-discogs-1) |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | [Reggae](/wiki/Reggae "Reggae")[[1]](#cite_note-discogs-1) |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | [Crab Records](/wiki/Crab_Records "Crab Records")[[1]](#cite_note-discogs-1) |
| [Songwriters](/wiki/Songwriter "Songwriter") | [Derrick Morgan](/wiki/Derrick_Morgan "Derrick Morgan") (Moon Hop)[[1]](#cite_note-discogs-1) [Joe Willis](/w/index.php?title=Joe_Willis_(songwriter)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Joe Willis (songwriter) (page does not exist)") (Harris Wheel)[[1]](#cite_note-discogs-1) |

"**Moon Hop**" is a 1969 single by [Derrick Morgan](/wiki/Derrick_Morgan "Derrick Morgan"). Backed with the [Reggaeites](/w/index.php?title=Reggaeites&action=edit&redlink=1 "Reggaeites (page does not exist)")' "Harris Wheel", it reached #49 on the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart"). The [British Afro-Caribbean](/wiki/British_Afro-Caribbean_community "British Afro-Caribbean community") [ska](/wiki/Ska "Ska") and [reggae](/wiki/Reggae "Reggae") band [Symarip](/wiki/Symarip "Symarip") covered "Moon Hop" as "Skinhead Moonstomp"; whilst unsuccessful on first release, in the wake of the [2 Tone](/wiki/2_Tone_(music_genre) "2 Tone (music genre)") revolution it was re-issued and charted at #54.

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Moon_Hop&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

"Moon Hop" was written to commemorate the July 20, 1969 landing of the [Apollo Lunar Module](/wiki/Apollo_Lunar_Module "Apollo Lunar Module") on the moon.[[2]](#cite_note-allmusic-2) Symarip's version was released shortly afterwards and many see a strong similarity between the two.[[2]](#cite_note-allmusic-2) The Symarip version includes a vocal introduction: "I want all you skinheads to get up on your feet/Put your braces together and your boots on your feet/And give me some of that old moonstomping" which was based on [Sam & Dave](/wiki/Sam_%26_Dave "Sam & Dave")'s "I Thank You.” The screeching guitar and lyrics were intended to appeal to [skinheads](/wiki/Skinhead "Skinhead"). When released for the first time, Moonstomp sold 5,000 copies, and Symarip named their album after it.[[2]](#cite_note-allmusic-2)

## Chart performance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Moon_Hop&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Chart performance")]

"Moon Hop" was the first version to chart on the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart"); backed with the [Reggaeites](/w/index.php?title=Reggaeites&action=edit&redlink=1 "Reggaeites (page does not exist)")' "Harris Wheel", it spent a week at #49 in January 1970.[[3]](#cite_note-British_Hit_Singles_&_Albums_379-3) Although [Symarip](/wiki/Symarip "Symarip")'s version didn't chart on first release, it was re-released ten years later in 1980 in the wake of the [2 Tone](/wiki/2_Tone_(music_genre) "2 Tone (music genre)") revolution, and spent three weeks on the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart") in 1980.[[4]](#cite_note-British_Hit_Singles_&_Albums_544-4)
